verses.text: At last the LORD answers Job out of the whirlwind: 'who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?' and bids him gird up and answer. God Himself takes up the case. When He speaks, every human argument falls silent.

At last the LORD answers Job out of the whirlwind: 'who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?' and bids him gird up and answer. God Himself takes up the case. When He speaks, every human argument falls silent.

At last the LORD answers Job out of the whirlwind: 'who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?' and bids him gird up and answer. God Himself takes up the case. When He speaks, every human argument falls silent.

'Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth?' God asks, when the morning stars sang together. Job was not there. The creature who questions the Creator was nowhere when the world was made.

Who shut the sea behind doors and said, 'hitherto shalt thou come, but no further'? God bounds the raging waters. The same hand that set the ocean's limits governs every flood that would overwhelm us.

Has Job ever commanded the morning or made the dawn seize the edges of the earth to shake out the wicked? The daily sunrise is God's doing. He who cannot summon one morning cannot govern the world.

Has Job walked the springs of the sea or seen the gates of death and the breadth of the earth? These depths are beyond him. The mysteries of creation lie far past the reach of human knowing.
Has Job walked the springs of the sea or seen the gates of death and the breadth of the earth? These depths are beyond him. The mysteries of creation lie far past the reach of human knowing.
Has Job walked the springs of the sea or seen the gates of death and the breadth of the earth? These depths are beyond him. The mysteries of creation lie far past the reach of human knowing.

Where does light dwell, and darkness? God asks with gentle irony, 'knowest thou it, because thou wast then born?' Job's brief life knows so little. Our few years cannot fathom the ancient ways of God.

Has Job entered the storehouses of snow and hail, which God keeps ready for the day of battle? The weather is His arsenal. He commands forces of nature that man can only watch in wonder.
Has Job entered the storehouses of snow and hail, which God keeps ready for the day of battle? The weather is His arsenal. He commands forces of nature that man can only watch in wonder.

Who channels the rain and the thunderbolt, watering even the empty wilderness where no man dwells? God's care reaches the loneliest waste. His goodness pours out where there is no one to thank Him.
Who channels the rain and the thunderbolt, watering even the empty wilderness where no man dwells? God's care reaches the loneliest waste. His goodness pours out where there is no one to thank Him.
Who channels the rain and the thunderbolt, watering even the empty wilderness where no man dwells? God's care reaches the loneliest waste. His goodness pours out where there is no one to thank Him.
Has the rain a father, or the frost and ice a human maker? They are born of God alone. The simplest drop and crystal come from a wisdom no man can claim.
Has the rain a father, or the frost and ice a human maker? They are born of God alone. The simplest drop and crystal come from a wisdom no man can claim.
Has the rain a father, or the frost and ice a human maker? They are born of God alone. The simplest drop and crystal come from a wisdom no man can claim.
Can Job bind the Pleiades or loose Orion, or rule the ordinances of heaven? The very stars obey laws he cannot touch. The God who orders the constellations is far beyond our managing.
Can Job bind the Pleiades or loose Orion, or rule the ordinances of heaven? The very stars obey laws he cannot touch. The God who orders the constellations is far beyond our managing.
Can Job bind the Pleiades or loose Orion, or rule the ordinances of heaven? The very stars obey laws he cannot touch. The God who orders the constellations is far beyond our managing.
Can Job summon the clouds, send the lightnings to report 'Here we are,' or give wisdom to the inward parts? All this is God's alone. Even the rain and the mind of man are His to give.

Does Job hunt prey for the lion or feed the young ravens that cry to God? The Maker provides for every creature. The God who feeds the wild beasts will surely not forget His own children.
